hypothesis: Introducing aromatic substitutions at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, improving selectivity margin over off-target subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The hypothesis is updated to prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved off-target interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5, while maintaining the pharmacophore for SSTR2 binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on these positions allows for targeted mutation to disrupt conserved pocket contacts.
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining overall peptide stability and pharmacophore integrity.
